steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student's goal is to define "georeferencing." The notes supply the definition directly: it is the process of assigning geographic coordinates to an image so that mapping software can place it in its real-world location. The correct choice must state what georeferencing is, not just describe a project that used it.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'A 2017 project by Tania López Marrero and colleagues assigned geographic coordinates to photographs of Puerto Rico's coastline and also used georeferencing.': Describes a specific project and incorrectly implies that assigning coordinates is separate from georeferencing; never defines the term itself. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Tania López Marrero and colleagues used georeferencing in their analysis of the earliest known aerial photographs of Puerto Rico.': Mentions that georeferencing was used but provides no definition of what it is. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Georeferenced aerial photographs from 1930 can help researchers analyze changes in Puerto Rico's coastline.': Explains a benefit of a georeferenced data set; does not define what georeferencing means. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Georeferencing is the process of assigning geographic coordinates to an image so that mapping software can place it in its real-world location.': Directly defines the term by naming both what the process does (assigns coordinates) and what it enables (placement in real-world location). ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
